topic: A student discovers their teacher follows them on social media
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification popped up, bright and unwelcome, on my phone screen. My breath hitched. It was a direct message from Mr. Harrison. My stomach clenched, a tight, cold knot forming beneath my ribs. I stared at the screen, my fingers trembling slightly as I read the carefully worded critique he’d posted about my recent essay. Every word felt magnified, scrutinized under the harsh glare of his digital gaze. I quickly minimized the app, shoving the phone into my pocket as if the device itself might burn me. Walking through the crowded hallway, I felt a strange stiffness in my shoulders, a desperate urge to become invisible, to melt into the linoleum floor. I kept my eyes fixed on the ground, acutely aware of every passing glance, convinced that the invisible tether of his attention was now a tangible, suffocating thing.

[story 2]

I found myself frozen mid-stride by my locker, the metal cool against my palm. The screen glowed with a picture of me, taken from a class project, captioned with a comment from Professor Evans. My cheeks burned, a hot flush creeping up my neck. I wanted to bolt, to disappear into the nearest shadow, but my feet felt cemented to the spot. I could feel the weight of everyone in the vicinity—the whispers, the sudden, sharp silence—all converging on this single, unwelcome exposure. A wave of intense awkwardness washed over me, making my throat dry and tight. I could feel my jaw clenching, a physical manifestation of the sheer discomfort of being so thoroughly observed, even in this mundane school setting.

[story 3]

It was late that evening when I stumbled upon it. Scrolling through my feed, I saw Mr. Davies’ profile picture. Then, a recent post—a slightly blurred photo from a field trip—and underneath it, a comment I had somehow missed until now. My hands started shaking uncontrollably. I dropped my phone onto my bedspread, the sound surprisingly loud in the quiet room. I lay there for a long time, the silence amplifying the frantic drumming in my chest. Every muscle in my body felt coiled and tense. When I finally forced myself to look back at the device, the image of his presence there felt like a spotlight, burning away any sense of peace I thought I possessed. I just wanted the noise of the world to stop, to erase the evidence of his intrusion.
